properties.remark: MULTIPLE REPORTS OF TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN IN THE STOUGHTON AREA.

NWS STORM SURVEY CONCLUDES THAT DAMAGE IN THE STOUGHTON AREA WAS DUE TO DOWNBURST/STRAIGHT LINE WINDS BETWEEN 70 AND 80 MPH. TREES, LIMBS AND POWER LINES WERE DOWNED AND A CAMPER WAS ROLLED. SOME TREES WERE DOWNED ON ROOFS, WITH A FEW HOMES SUSTAINING DAMAGE TO SHINGLES AND FACIA. THE GREATEST CONCENTRATION OF DAMAGE WAS OBSERVED IN SOUTHWEST STOUGHTON.

NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE STORM SURVEY CONFIRMS A TORNADO RATED EF-1 WITH A MAX WIND SPEED OF AROUND 95MPH AND A MAX WIDTH OF AROUND 50 YARDS. THE PATH OF THE TORNADO BEGINS SOUTH OF LESLIE ROAD WEST OF VEIUM ROAD WHERE HOMES AND OUTBUILDINGS SUSTAINED DAMAGE. THE TRACK QUICKLY MOVES NORTHEAST WITH ADDITIONAL DAMAGE TO A FEW HOMES AND OUTBUILDINGS ALONG WITH DOWNED TREES AND TREE LIMBS. THE TRACK ENDS NEAR HIGHWAY W WEST OF HIGHWAY A. TOTAL TRACK TIME IS APPROXIMATELY 4 MINUTES WITH A LENGTH OF APPROXIMATELY 5 MILES.
EIGHTEEN 40 FOOT PINE TREES SHEARED OFF ABOUT 15 FEET ABOVE THE GROUND. SEVERAL OTHER TREES DOWN NEARBY. A TREE LANDED ON A GARAGE PIERCING THE ROOF.
ESTIMATED GUST OF 60 MPH WINDS AS STORMS MOVED THROUGH NEAR HWY26 & HWY16.
